title: Tinantia anomala
intro: Tinantia anomala is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Mexico, commonly known as "Mexican coffee" due to its coffee-like seeds. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tinantia anomala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tinantia anomala is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is also used in some commercial supplements to support immune function and overall well-being.
